title: Downed Power Line Field-Operations SOP
content: Treat every downed power line as energized until engineering confirms isolation. Establish a 30-foot exclusion zone, keep reporters and field units outside it, and request immediate utility isolation. If there is fire, injury, blocked emergency access, or a line on a vehicle or structure, classify as SEV1 and escalate to the escalations queue while field-operations coordinates with public safety. Record location, arcing, pole or transformer identifier if available, and unit status codes. Do not announce all-clear until the line is de-energized, hazards are removed, and the SLA clock is satisfied.
snippet: Field-operations SOP for downed power line isolation, SEV1 triggers, and all-clear conditions.

title: Gas Odor and Leak Response SOP
content: Signals describing rotten egg odor, hissing near a meter, dizziness near gas equipment, or visible vapor require immediate life-safety review. Open an incident for field-operations; set severity to SEV1 when odor is indoors, near a school, near a medical facility, or accompanied by illness, fire, or explosion indicators. Field units should stage upwind, avoid ignition sources, and coordinate with public safety dispatch before entering the area. A sitrep must cite the signal, this SOP, and any unit status updates used for factual claims.
snippet: Gas odor SOP covering rotten egg reports, SEV1 escalation, staging, and citation grounding.

title: Water Main Break Field Procedure
content: For water-main-break signals, capture street intersection, visible flow direction, sinkhole indicators, and whether service is impaired for critical facilities. Assign field-operations unless engineering telemetry shows a pressure-zone fault. Use SEV2 for spreading roadway impact or multiple affected blocks, SEV1 if the break blocks emergency access, undermines a structure, or creates a statutory water-quality clock. Do not state restoration time unless field-operations or engineering has published it as a source record.
snippet: Water main break procedure for field assessment, severity, and restoration-time discipline.
